No signs of obfuscation, secrets, or malicious code.\n- **Malware Score:** All reports agree on 0, which is correct. The code does not perform any malicious actions.\n- **Obfuscated Score:** All reports 0, correct.\n- **Risk Score:** The highest is 0.1 (Report 5), which is reasonable considering the code's role as an interface wrapper. It does not introduce security risks by itself but could be used maliciously if connected to a malicious contract, which is outside the scope of this code snippet.\n\n### Final Justification:\n\n- The code is purely a static contract interface factory, a common pattern in blockchain development.\n- No dynamic code, no user input processing, no external network calls beyond contract interaction.\n- No embedded secrets, backdoors, or obfuscation.\n- The small risk score (0.1) in the last report is acceptable, reflecting minimal inherent risk in the wrapper code itself.\n\n---\n\n### **Final Recommendation:**\n\n**Malware Score:** 0  \n**Obfuscated Score:** 0  \n**Risk Score:** 0.1 (consistent with the last report, acceptable for a standard interface wrapper)\n\n**Overall, this code is safe, standard, and does not pose any security threat.**",
